items.title: In-Place Test-Time Training
items.authors: Guhao Feng, Shengjie Luo, Kai Hua et al.
items.abstract: The static ``train then deploy" paradigm fundamentally limits Large Language Models (LLMs) from dynamically adapting their weights in response to continuous streams of new information inherent in real-world tasks. Test-Time Training (TTT) offers a compelling alternative by updating a subset of model...

items.title: DiffHDR: Re-Exposing LDR Videos with Video Diffusion Models
items.authors: Zhengming Yu, Li Ma, Mingming He et al.
items.abstract: Most digital videos are stored in 8-bit low dynamic range (LDR) formats, where much of the original high dynamic range (HDR) scene radiance is lost due to saturation and quantization. This loss of highlight and shadow detail precludes mapping accurate luminance to HDR displays and limits meaningful ...

items.title: Paper Circle: An Open-source Multi-agent Research Discovery and Analysis Framework
items.authors: Komal Kumar, Aman Chadha, Salman Khan et al.
items.abstract: The rapid growth of scientific literature has made it increasingly difficult for researchers to efficiently discover, evaluate, and synthesize relevant work. Recent advances in multi-agent large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong potential for understanding user intent and are being tra...

items.title: Action Images: End-to-End Policy Learning via Multiview Video Generation
items.authors: Haoyu Zhen, Zixian Gao, Qiao Sun et al.
items.abstract: World action models (WAMs) have emerged as a promising direction for robot policy learning, as they can leverage powerful video backbones to model the future states. However, existing approaches often rely on separate action modules, or use action representations that are not pixel-grounded, making ...

items.title: HaloProbe: Bayesian Detection and Mitigation of Object Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
items.authors: Reihaneh Zohrabi, Hosein Hasani, Akshita Gupta et al.
items.abstract: Large vision-language models can produce object hallucinations in image descriptions, highlighting the need for effective detection and mitigation strategies. Prior work commonly relies on the model's attention weights on visual tokens as a detection signal. We reveal that coarse-grained attention-b...

items.title: Topological Characterization of Churn Flow and Unsupervised Correction to the Wu Flow-Regime Map in Small-Diameter Vertical Pipes
items.authors: Brady Koenig, Sushovan Majhi, Atish Mitra et al.
items.abstract: Churn flow-the chaotic, oscillatory regime in vertical two-phase flow-has lacked a quantitative mathematical definition for over $40$ years. We introduce the first topology-based characterization using Euler Characteristic Surfaces (ECS). We formulate unsupervised regime discovery as Multiple Kernel...

items.title: Delta6: A Low-Cost, 6-DOF Force-Sensing Flexible End-Effector
items.authors: Yue Feng, Weicheng Huang, Chen Qiu et al.
items.abstract: This paper presents Delta6, a low-cost, six-degree-of-freedom (6-DOF) force/torque end-effector that combines antagonistic springs with magnetic encoders to deliver accurate wrench sensing while remaining as simple to assemble as flat-pack furniture. A fully 3D-printed prototype, assembled entirely ...
